scenarioMeta.name: 2008 US Housing Crash
scenarioMeta.description: Collapse of the housing market and global banking crisis.

sectors.name: Technology
sectors.description: Resilient balance sheets allowed for rapid post-crisis growth.

companies.name: Amazon
companies.financialHealth: Revenue growing rapidly, but margins still thin. Low debt.
companies.bullCase: E-commerce is the future of retail, and AWS is a hidden gem.
companies.bearCase: Valuation is too high, and consumer spending will drop in a recession.

companies.name: Apple
companies.financialHealth: Strong cash position, zero debt, explosive revenue growth.
companies.bullCase: The iPhone is a revolutionary product with massive addressable market.
companies.bearCase: Consumer discretionary spending will plummet, hurting premium products.

sectors.name: Banking
sectors.description: Systemic risk and toxic assets led to massive write-downs.

companies.name: Citigroup
companies.financialHealth: Massive exposure to toxic CDOs, requiring multiple government bailouts.
companies.bullCase: Too big to fail, government will backstop losses.
companies.bearCase: Insolvent without government intervention, equity will be diluted.

companies.name: JPMorgan
companies.financialHealth: Fortress balance sheet, avoided the worst of the subprime mess.
companies.bullCase: Will emerge stronger by acquiring weaker rivals (Bear Stearns, WaMu).
companies.bearCase: Systemic risk could drag down even the strongest banks.

companies.name: Lehman Brothers
companies.description: Global investment bank heavily exposed to subprime mortgages.
companies.financialHealth: Massive leverage (30:1), huge exposure to toxic real estate assets.
companies.bullCase: The government will orchestrate a rescue like Bear Stearns.
companies.bearCase: The firm is insolvent and counterparties are pulling back.
companies.collapseLesson: Excessive leverage and reliance on short-term funding for illiquid assets.

sectors.name: Energy
sectors.description: Oil spiked then crashed with global demand.

companies.name: Exxon
companies.financialHealth: Record profits in early 2008, strong balance sheet.
companies.bullCase: Global demand for energy will continue to rise long-term.
companies.bearCase: Oil prices are crashing as global recession destroys demand.

companies.name: J&J
companies.financialHealth: AAA credit rating, steady cash flows, strong dividend.
companies.bullCase: Defensive stock, people still need healthcare in a recession.

companies.name: Simon Prop
companies.financialHealth: High debt levels typical of REITs, facing refinancing risks.
companies.bullCase: High-quality assets will survive the downturn.
companies.bearCase: Retailers going bankrupt will lead to high vacancy rates.

companies.name: Walmart
companies.financialHealth: Strong cash flow, counter-cyclical business model.
companies.bullCase: Consumers will trade down to discount retailers in a recession.
companies.bearCase: Even discount retail will suffer if unemployment spikes too high.

companies.name: GE
companies.financialHealth: Heavy reliance on GE Capital for profits, facing severe liquidity crisis.
companies.bearCase: GE Capital is a black box of risk, dividend is in danger.

companies.name: BHP
companies.financialHealth: Strong cash flows from China boom, but commodity prices crashing.
companies.bullCase: China's stimulus will revive commodity demand.
companies.bearCase: Global recession will destroy demand for industrial metals.

news.title: Lehman Brothers Files for Bankruptcy
news.summary: The 158-year-old firm collapses, triggering a global financial panic. Markets are in freefall.

news.title: AIG Seeks Federal Bailout
news.summary: Insurance giant faces liquidity crisis due to credit default swaps.

news.title: Gold Prices Surge
news.summary: Investors flee to safety as equities plummet.

news.title: Fed Injects Liquidity
news.summary: Central banks coordinate to keep credit markets functioning.

news.title: Congress Passes TARP
news.summary: The $700 billion Troubled Asset Relief Program is signed into law.

news.title: Global Markets Plunge
news.summary: Despite bailouts, fear grips international exchanges.

news.title: Warren Buffett Invests in Goldman
news.summary: Berkshire Hathaway injects $5 billion into the investment bank.

news.title: Unemployment Rises Sharply
news.summary: Job losses mount as the recession deepens.

news.title: Fed Announces QE1
news.summary: The Federal Reserve will purchase $600 billion in mortgage-backed securities.

news.title: Auto Industry Begs for Help
news.summary: Big Three automakers plead with Congress for emergency loans.

news.title: Consumer Spending Plummets
news.summary: Retailers report worst holiday season start in decades.

news.title: China Announces Stimulus
news.summary: A $586 billion infrastructure package to combat global slowdown.

news.title: Madoff Ponzi Scheme Exposed
news.summary: $65 billion fraud shakes investor confidence further.

news.title: Fed Cuts Rates to Zero
news.summary: Unprecedented move to a target range of 0 to 0.25%.

news.title: Treasury Yields Hit Record Lows
news.summary: Flight to safety pushes 10-year yield below 2.1%.

news.title: OPEC Cuts Production
news.summary: Cartel slashes output by 2.2 million barrels a day to halt price slide.

news.title: Obama Inaugurated
news.summary: New administration promises swift action on economic recovery.

news.title: Bank of America Absorbs Merrill Losses
news.summary: Government provides additional $20 billion to stabilize BofA.

news.title: Job Losses Top 600,000
news.summary: Worst single-month job loss since 1974.

news.title: Tech Earnings Disappoint
news.summary: Major tech firms report steep declines in enterprise spending.

news.title: Stimulus Bill Signed
news.summary: The $787 billion American Recovery and Reinvestment Act becomes law.

news.title: Fears of Bank Nationalization
news.summary: Rumors swirl that the government may take over Citigroup and BofA.

news.title: Housing Prices Hit Bottom
news.summary: Case-Shiller index shows record 19% year-over-year drop.

news.title: Warren Buffett's Warning
news.summary: Oracle of Omaha says economy is in 'shambles' but remains long-term optimistic.

news.title: Market Hits Generational Bottom
news.summary: S&P 500 touches 666 as sentiment reaches extreme pessimism.

news.title: Fed Expands QE
news.summary: Central bank will buy $300 billion in long-term Treasuries.

news.title: Banks Report Profitable Start to Year
news.summary: Citigroup and JPMorgan signal a turn in fortunes.

news.title: Geithner Unveils Toxic Asset Plan
news.summary: Public-Private Investment Program aims to clear bank balance sheets.

investorDecisions.name: Warren Buffett
investorDecisions.philosophy: Value investing, long horizon, avoids leverage

investorDecisions.name: George Soros
investorDecisions.philosophy: Macro bets, reflexivity theory, high conviction short positions

investorDecisions.name: Peter Lynch
investorDecisions.philosophy: Growth at reasonable price, retail insights, sector rotation

lessonsTriggered.title: The Power of Diversification
lessonsTriggered.content: When a single sector like Real Estate crashes, having investments in Healthcare or Consumer Staples can buffer your portfolio. This is why legendary investors rarely put all their eggs in one basket.

lessonsTriggered.title: Catching a Falling Knife
lessonsTriggered.content: Buying an asset simply because it has fallen sharply can be dangerous. Ensure the underlying fundamentals justify the purchase, rather than just the price drop.

lessonsTriggered.title: Cash is a Position
lessonsTriggered.content: In times of extreme volatility, holding cash is an active investment decision. It preserves capital and provides ammunition to buy assets when prices become attractive.

legendaryQuotes.quote: Be fearful when others are greedy, and greedy when others are fearful.
The worse a situation becomes, the less it takes to turn it around, and the bigger the upside.
You get recessions, you have stock market declines. If you don't understand that's going to happen, then you're not ready, you won't do well in the markets.
He who lives by the crystal ball will eat shattered glass.
